items.prompt: What does a living documentation checklist emphasize?
choices: Updating metadata, dictionaries, codebooks, provenance, limits, and access terms on every release
Writing documentation only once at project kickoff
Deleting older notes after each model ships
Storing docs only in personal chat history
items.explain: Releases stay usable when the same minimum fields are maintained as data change.

items.prompt: A commit message like “Added cleaned training dataset with missing values imputed using median” is preferred over a vague “update.”
Example 8.34’s recommended response to large binaries is to:
choices: Force all binaries into every Git commit blob forever
Use Git LFS or DVC to store large files externally and track metadata in Git
items.explain: External storage with tracked metadata keeps repositories usable.

items.prompt: Give one naming or folder convention that helps collaborators identify dataset state.
items.rubric: Mentions structured filenames (project/version/date) or separating raw/processed/models/outputs.
items.sample_answer: Use names like project_v2_2024-12-01 and keep raw, processed, and model folders separate.
